name: a-theory-of-justice
description: John Rawls' 'A Theory of Justice' — the most influential work of political philosophy of the 20th century. Rawls proposes a theory of 'justice as fairness' based on the original position behind a veil of ignorance, from which rational individuals would choose two principles: the equal liberty principle and the difference principle. Covers reflective equilibrium, civil disobedience, the priority of liberty, fair equality of opportunity, and the stability of a just society.
